name: Archmage Nutkin

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.description: The squirrel has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

traits.name: Nimble Escape
traits.description: The squirrel can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action.

traits.name: Keen Smell
traits.description: The squirrel has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: Archmage Nutkin makes two attacks with Arcane Acorn or casts a cantrip and makes one Arcane Acorn attack.

actions.name: Arcane Acorn
actions.description: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6+5) force damage.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d4+4) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

actions.name: Cone of Acorns
actions.description: The squirrel unleashes a barrage of magically-propelled acorns in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 (8d6) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

bonusActions.name: Misty Hop
bonusActions.description: The squirrel magically teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

bonusActions.name: Acorn Shield
bonusActions.description: The squirrel summons a swirling shield of enchanted acorns, gaining a +2 bonus to AC until the start of its next turn.

bonusActions.name: Quick Cast
bonusActions.description: The squirrel casts a cantrip with a casting time of 1 action.

reactions.name: Shield
reactions.description: When hit by an attack, the squirrel gains a +5 bonus to AC until the start of its next turn, including against the triggering attack.

reactions.name: Counterspell
reactions.description: When a creature within 60 feet casts a spell, the squirrel can attempt to counter it. The squirrel makes an Intelligence check (DC 10 + spell level). On a success, the spell fails.

reactions.name: Tail Deflection
reactions.description: When the squirrel would be hit by a ranged weapon attack, it can use its bushy tail to deflect the projectile, adding 3 to its AC against that attack.

legendary.description: Archmage Nutkin can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The squirrel regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

actions.name: Detect
actions.description: The squirrel makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

actions.name: Acorn Blast
actions.description: The squirrel makes one Arcane Acorn attack.

actions.name: Scurry
actions.description: The squirrel moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

actions.name: Cast Spell
actions.description: The squirrel casts a spell of 3rd level or lower from its spell list.

description: A small but impossibly powerful squirrel wrapped in robes of autumn leaves. Its eyes sparkle with arcane power, and it carries a staff topped with a glowing acorn. Nutkin speaks in a high-pitched, rapid voice and gestures dramatically when casting spells. Despite its formidable magical prowess, it remains utterly obsessed with collecting and organizing acorns.
tactics: Nutkin opens combat with Cone of Acorns if enemies are clustered, then uses Misty Hop to maintain distance. It favors ranged Arcane Acorn attacks and high-level spell slots for fireball or polymorph. Uses Shield and Counterspell reactions liberally. If bloodied, casts greater invisibility and retreats to a high vantage point. Legendary actions prioritize Acorn Blast for damage and Scurry for repositioning. Never fights to the death—will teleport away if reduced below 20 HP.
lootNotes: The Ancient World Tree Acorn is a key quest item. The Staff of Eternal Acorns functions as a +2 quarterstaff that can summon 1d4 goodberries once per day. The Robe of Autumn Leaves grants advantage on Stealth checks in forested areas.
